seo.title: RabbitMQ Message Spy — Inspect Payloads Without Consuming | Qarote
seo.description: Peek inside live RabbitMQ queues and inspect message headers and payloads without consuming them. Read-only, safe for production. No throwaway consumer scripts.

hero.title: Inspect Production Messages Without Consuming Them
hero.subtitle: Browse message headers and payloads directly inside any queue — read-only, non-destructive, safe for production. No AMQP consumer. No throwaway scripts. The messages stay exactly where they are.

problem.heading: Debugging queue contents today means touching the queue
problem.body: The RabbitMQ management plugin's basic_get can be destructive (destructive by default unless requeue/ackmode is set) — without requeue configured, it consumes and acknowledges the message. Writing a debug consumer just to inspect payloads is error-prone, slow, and risky in production. Neither option gives you a clean read-only view of what's sitting in your queues.

items.title: basic_get is destructive by default
items.description: The management plugin's message browser uses basic_get under the hood. Without requeue/ackmode configured, the message is consumed. If your consumer requeue logic isn't perfect, you've just lost a message.

items.title: Debug consumers are overhead
items.description: Writing a throwaway AMQP consumer to inspect payloads means deploying code to production, managing credentials, and hoping you don't accidentally process the messages you're trying to inspect.

items.title: Schema mismatches are invisible
items.description: When a new publisher deploys with a different payload structure, your consumers start failing — but you can't verify what's actually in the queue without consuming a message.

solution.heading: Read-only peek. Real payload. Zero risk.
solution.body: Message Spy uses the RabbitMQ HTTP API's non-destructive preview endpoint — not basic_get, not AMQP consume. Messages are fetched as a read-only snapshot. Your consumers see nothing. The queue state is unchanged.

steps.title: Open any queue
steps.description: Navigate to any queue in your Qarote dashboard. Message Spy is available on all queues you have read access to.

steps.title: Click Spy
steps.description: Qarote fetches a preview batch via the non-destructive HTTP API endpoint. No AMQP connection opened. No messages consumed or acknowledged.

steps.title: Browse headers and payload
steps.description: Inspect routing key, exchange, timestamp, delivery mode, and full payload inline. JSON is syntax-highlighted. Binary payloads are hex-displayed. Copy raw content in one click.

items.title: Message payload
items.description: Full body content — JSON formatted and syntax-highlighted, plain text displayed as-is, binary payloads shown as hex. Copy raw content or formatted output.

items.title: Headers and properties
items.description: Routing key, exchange, delivery mode, priority, correlation ID, reply-to, expiration, content type, and all custom headers set by the publisher.

items.title: Message position
items.description: Position of each message in the queue (first, second, third…) and approximate age based on timestamp. Understand ordering and staleness at a glance.

items.title: Redelivery status
items.description: Whether a message has been redelivered — a key indicator that consumers are failing to process it. Spot poison messages before they saturate your consumers.

useCases.heading: The moments Message Spy is built for

items.title: Production debugging
items.description: Consumers are failing and you don't know why. Open the queue, inspect the payload — is it malformed? Wrong schema? Missing field? Answer in 10 seconds without touching consumers.

items.title: Pre-deploy schema validation
items.description: A new publisher just deployed. Before your consumers go live, verify the payload structure is what you expect. Catch schema mismatches before they become incidents.

items.title: Poison message detection
items.description: A message with redelivered: true has been rejected multiple times. Inspect it directly to understand why — without writing a dead-letter consumer.

items.title: Integration testing
items.description: Verify that your publisher is sending correctly formatted messages to the right queues with the right routing keys — without running a consumer in your test environment.

safety.heading: Non-destructive by design
safety.body: Message Spy uses the RabbitMQ HTTP API's /get endpoint with requeue=true and count=N. This is the same mechanism the management plugin uses for its built-in message browser — with one critical difference: Qarote always sets requeue=true, ensuring messages are never consumed. Access is gated by Qarote's permission model: users without queue read access cannot spy. On self-hosted deployments, your RabbitMQ credentials never leave your infrastructure.

items.question: Is Message Spy safe to use in production?
items.answer: Yes. Qarote uses the RabbitMQ HTTP API with requeue=true — messages are never consumed or acknowledged. Your consumers are completely unaffected. The queue state is identical before and after a spy operation.

items.question: Is this the same as the management plugin's message browser?
items.answer: The underlying mechanism is the same RabbitMQ HTTP API endpoint. Qarote adds non-destructive enforcement (always requeue=true), syntax-highlighted JSON rendering, full header tree display, redelivery status, and integration with the rest of your queue dashboard — no tab-switching.

items.question: How many messages can I inspect at once?
items.answer: Developer plans can inspect up to 10 messages per queue per request. Enterprise plans have a configurable batch size. Messages are shown in queue order (FIFO).

items.question: Does Message Spy work with encrypted payloads?
items.answer: Message Spy displays the raw payload as Qarote receives it from the broker. If your application encrypts message bodies before publishing, the payload will appear encrypted. Qarote does not decrypt message content.

items.question: What about sensitive data in payloads?
items.answer: Access to Message Spy is controlled by Qarote's user permission model. Only users with explicit queue read access can spy on that queue. On self-hosted deployments, you control the entire authentication layer.

items.question: Does Message Spy work with all RabbitMQ versions?
items.answer: Yes — Message Spy uses the standard RabbitMQ HTTP management API, available in all supported versions (3.x and 4.x).
